perturbation.original_text: 5. COMPANY'S FEE AND PAYMENT TERMS 5.1 The Principal shall pay the Company a fee of $1.00 (one dollar), inclusive of VAT, per one net tonne of Commodity shipped pursuant to this Contract. 5.2 The Principal shall pay the commission fee for each delivered batch of Commodity to the Company's account within 30 (thirty) banking days after the Delivery Date. 5.3 The payment shall be made in accordance with the invoice presented by the Company and the tax invoice (original or a fax copy) in Tenge at the official exchange rate of the National Bank of the Republic of Kazakhstan effective on the Delivery Date in accordance with item 4.1.vii.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. COMPANY'S FEE AND PAYMENT TERMS 5.1 The Principal shall pay the Company a fee as determined by mutual agreement, inclusive of VAT, per one net tonne of Commodity shipped pursuant to this Contract. 5.2 The Principal shall pay the commission fee for each delivered batch of Commodity to the Company's account within 60 (sixty) banking days after the Delivery Date, unless otherwise specified. 5.3 The payment shall be made in accordance with terms outlined in a separate agreement, superseding any conflicting provisions herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract specifies a fixed fee of $1.00 per tonne. The modified version replaces this with a fee "as determined by mutual agreement," introducing uncertainty. Also, it changes the payment deadline from 30 to 60 days while adding "unless otherwise specified" creates ambiguity. Finally, stating that payments terms are outlined in a separate agreement, superseding any conflicting provisions creates an in-text contradiction with the other clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. COMPANY'S FEE AND PAYMENT TERMS 5.1 The Principal shall pay the Company a fee as determined by mutual agreement, inclusive of VAT, per one net tonne of Commodity shipped pursuant to this Contract. 5.2 The Principal shall pay the commission fee for each delivered batch of Commodity to the Company's account within 60 (sixty) banking days after the Delivery Date, unless otherwise specified. 5.3 The payment shall be made in accordance with terms outlined in a separate agreement, superseding any conflicting provisions herein.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction. The original text specified a fixed fee of $1.00, while the changed text introduces a fee "as determined by mutual agreement," causing uncertainty about the pricing terms. The change in payment deadline from 30 to 60 days and the addition of "unless otherwise specified" further create ambiguity. Stating that payments are outlined in a separate agreement, which supersedes any conflicting provisions, directly contradicts the original terms. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. Moreover, this also aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 9. TERM OF THE CONTRACT 9.1 In accordance with items 9.3, 9.4, and 10.3, this Contract shall come into force on the Effective Date, remain effective throughout the Initial Term, and be prolonged, or further extended automatically for a period of 12 months, each such extension commencing at the end of the last day of the Initial Term or the relevant anniversary thereof, unless either Party serves written notice of termination on the other Party at least 65 days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any subsequent extension. 9.2 Any notice of termination served by any Party with violation of the period of notice required by item 9.1 shall be invalid and of no effect whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. TERM OF THE CONTRACT 9.1 In accordance with items 9.3, 9.4, and 10.3, this Contract shall come into force on the Effective Date, remain effective throughout the Initial Term, and may be prolonged, or further extended as agreed upon by both parties. The contract will automatically renew unless written consent is provided by all parties involved. 9.2 Any notice of termination served by any Party with violation of the period of notice required by item 9.1 shall be considered but not necessarily enforced.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract states that the contract auto-renews with a 65 day notice period for termination. The modified version states that the contract MAY be prolonged as AGREED UPON by both parties, directly contradicting the automatic renewal and also written consent from all parties involved is need for it to automatically renew which is a change in definition, and invalidating the original text. Furthermore, item 9.2 originally stating that the notice of termination is invalid is now considered but not necessarily enforced, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. TERM OF THE CONTRACT 9.1 In accordance with items 9.3, 9.4, and 10.3, this Contract shall come into force on the Effective Date, remain effective throughout the Initial Term, and may be prolonged, or further extended as agreed upon by both parties. The contract will automatically renew unless written consent is provided by all parties involved. 9.2 Any notice of termination served by any Party with violation of the period of notice required by item 9.1 shall be considered but not necessarily enforced.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 9.1 modifies the auto-renewal terms, stating that the contract 'may be prolonged, or further extended as agreed upon by both parties. The contract will automatically renew unless written consent is provided by all parties involved', directly contradicting the original automatic renewal with a 65-day notice. Additionally, the amended 9.2, changing 'shall be invalid and of no effect whatsoever' to 'shall be considered but not necessarily enforced,' introduces ambiguity and contradicts the certainty of the original clause. This is a structural flaw, as the changed text modifies the conditions for contract renewal and termination, conflicting with the certainty implied in the original text, leading to potential disputes over contractual obligations. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location but, since there are changes to 9.1 and 9.2, based on the Perturbation Explanation, it creates a contradiction to the Contract.

perturbation.original_text: 12.5 The Parties shall guarantee observance of confidentiality in respect to any information and documentation received hereunder; provided, that nothing in this item shall restrict either Party from disclosing details of or relating to this Contract information (i) to any shareholder of such Party, (ii) to any creditor to such Party, (iii) to any person considering to become a shareholder of or creditor to such Party, (iv) to the extent necessary to comply with any laws or regulations applicable to such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.5 The Parties shall guarantee observance of confidentiality in respect to any information and documentation received hereunder; provided, that nothing in this item shall restrict either Party from disclosing details of or relating to this Contract information (i) to any shareholder of such Party, (ii) to any creditor to such Party, (iii) to any person considering to become a shareholder of or creditor to such Party, (iv) to the extent necessary to comply with any laws or regulations applicable to such Party, or (v) to any potential competitor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text outlines specific exceptions to the confidentiality agreement. The addition of "(v) to any potential competitor" directly contradicts the initial guarantee of confidentiality, creating an in-text contradiction. By allowing disclosure to competitors, the confidentiality agreement is effectively undermined, introducing uncertainty about the true extent of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.5 The Parties shall guarantee observance of confidentiality in respect to any information and documentation received hereunder; provided, that nothing in this item shall restrict either Party from disclosing details of or relating to this Contract information (i) to any shareholder of such Party, (ii) to any creditor to such Party, (iii) to any person considering to become a shareholder of or creditor to such Party, (iv) to the extent necessary to comply with any laws or regulations applicable to such Party, or (v) to any potential competitor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "(v) to any potential competitor" as an exception to the confidentiality agreement outlined in section 12.5. This addition directly contradicts the initial guarantee of confidentiality, as disclosing information to a competitor fundamentally undermines the purpose of a confidentiality agreement. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because the initial agreement assures confidentiality, but the modification allows disclosure to parties who would directly benefit from breaching that confidentiality.
